email.label: Email address
email.passwordRequired: Current password required to change your email.

feedback.taken: This email is already in use.

recoveryCode.title: Recovery code
recoveryCode.descriptionSet: A 12-word BIP39 code lets you recover your account if you forget your password — without losing your data. Regenerating immediately invalidates the previous code.
recoveryCode.descriptionUnset: Without this code, forgetting your password means losing all your data. 12 BIP39 words to write down once; you'll be able to recover your account with them, without destroying your entries.

password.title: Password
password.description: Re-derives your key on a dedicated page — the master key is re-wrapped locally before reaching the server, with no loss of your encrypted entries. The admin never sees it.

totp.title: 2FA (TOTP)
totp.descriptionEnabledTail: You can manage your backup codes or disable 2FA from this screen.
totp.descriptionDisabled: A six-digit code on every login, generated by an authenticator app (Bitwarden, Ente Auth, Aegis, Google Auth) on top of your password — a leak alone is no longer enough to get in.

passkey.title: Passkey
passkey.descriptionEmpty: A passkey (Touch ID, Face ID, Windows Hello, Yubikey, password manager…) replaces typing your password at login. If it's PRF-capable it also decrypts your data — otherwise it logs you in but still asks for your password.
passkey.descriptionExtras: You can add more or remove the ones you no longer use.

mode.title: Security mode
mode.description: How many factors are required at each login (password, TOTP, passkey). Current mode: {mode}.

sessions.title: Active sessions
sessions.description: Devices currently signed in to your account. You can revoke a specific session (the device will be logged out immediately) or sign out everywhere at once.
sessions.revokeConfirm: You're about to revoke the {device} session. That device will be signed out immediately and will need to log back in with its password.
sessions.revokeStaleAuth: Re-enter your password to revoke a session.
sessions.logoutAllConfirm: You're about to be signed out on every device, including this one. Continue?

preferences.themeDescription: Light, dark, or follows your system.
preferences.backgroundShadeDescription: Five warm shades chosen to harmonise with Nodea's sage, for light mode.
preferences.backgroundShadeDarkDescription: Five dark surfaces, from warm night-paper to a neutral near-black.

modules.intro1: Every module is enabled by default.
modules.intro2: Disable the ones you don't use — they'll vanish from the sidebar and their data will be left intact (nothing is deleted).

phraseGate.title: Backup phrase
phraseGate.intro: A 12-word phrase encrypts all your backups (manual and automatic). Shown the first time you create an encrypted one — save it, without it your files are permanently unrecoverable.
phraseGate.confirmedDescription: This phrase encrypts your manual and automatic backups. You can reset it if needed — backups already made keep their old phrase.

export.title: Export
export.description: Two formats: an encrypted backup (.age, recommended) or a plain export (.json, GDPR). Everything is generated in your browser, nothing goes to the server.
export.partial: Partial export — these modules could not be exported and are missing from the file: {modules}. Try again for a complete backup.

import.title: Import
import.description: Encrypted .age backup (you'll be asked for the 12 words), or a JSON / NDJSON export, from Nodea. Duplicates are ignored. Note: links between records (reviews ↔ books, HRT intakes ↔ schedules) are not preserved when restoring into a different account.
import.moduleFailed: {key}: failed — interrupted, run again to finish
import.orphanedRefs: {count} item(s) restored without their parent link (parent missing from the backup)
import.backupDetected: Encrypted backup detected. Enter the 12 words to restore it.
import.backupWrongPassphrase: Wrong passphrase, or the backup file was altered.
import.backupPhraseHint: Separate the words with spaces or hyphens; case doesn't matter.
import.backupPhrasePlaceholder: Paste or type your 12 words here.

backup.partial: Partial backup — these modules could not be included: {modules}. Try again for a complete backup.
backup.phraseSubtitle: Write down these 12 words: they decrypt your backup. You'll confirm a few before the download.
backup.phraseSubtitleConfirm: Write down these 12 words: they decrypt your backups. You'll confirm a few of them.
backup.phraseWarningBody: These 12 words decrypt your .age backup — they won't be shown again. Without them, the file is permanently unrecoverable.
backup.reusedMessage: Your backup phrase already exists — we reuse the same one. Confirm it below with the quiz. You can regenerate it if needed, but that only applies to future backups (existing ones keep their phrase).
backup.regenerateConfirm: Backups you've already created will keep their old phrase; only future ones will use the new phrase. Be sure to write down the new phrase on the next screen.

cloudBackup.title: Cloud service
cloudBackup.description: Connect Dropbox, pCloud or Nextcloud: your encrypted backup is sent there automatically (at most once a day), and you can restore from it. The .age file is encrypted in your browser — the service only ever sees an unreadable blob.
cloudBackup.connectedDescription: {provider} connected. Encrypted backup sent automatically (at most once a day); you can restore from it under “Restore”.

restore.title: Restore this backup?
restore.message: A backup already exists at this destination. Restore it now? Your current data won't be overwritten — we only add what's missing.
restore.manualHint: Couldn't auto-decrypt (a backup from another account?). Use “Restore from cloud” with the 12 words.
restore.partialHint: Restore incomplete. Re-run it from “Restore from cloud” to finish before the next auto-backup.
restore.uncheckedHint: Couldn't check the destination. If a backup exists, restore it from “Restore from cloud” before the next auto-backup.

cloudRestore.title: From the cloud
cloudRestore.description: Fetches and merges the backup found on {provider}. Non-destructive: nothing is overwritten, only what's missing is added.
cloudRestore.none: No backup found at this destination.
cloudRestore.upToDate: Already up to date — nothing new to restore.
cloudRestore.phrasePrompt: Couldn't auto-decrypt. Enter this backup's 12 words.

webdav.serverHint: Server address or WebDAV URL, with or without “https://” — it all works.
webdav.appPasswordWarn: Never use your Nextcloud account password.
webdav.appPasswordHint: Nextcloud → Settings → Security → Devices & sessions → “Create new app password”.
webdav.appPasswordLink: Open that screen on your Nextcloud
webdav.folderHint: Optional — empty = root. The folder is created if it doesn't exist.

error.cors: Connection blocked. On Nextcloud, the “webapppassword” app must be installed and Nodea’s address allowed (origins); also check the server URL.
error.auth: Credentials rejected. Check the username and app password (and that its filesystem access is enabled).
error.path: Unexpected server response. Check the URL and username.

danger.heading: Account deletion
danger.emailMismatch: This doesn’t match your account email.
danger.gateHint: Re-type your email and current password to enable deletion.
danger.windowConfirm: This action is irreversible. All your encrypted entries will be deleted. Continue?
danger.explanation: Deletion erases all your encrypted entries, sessions and invites. No recovery possible — consider exporting first.
